title: Elaphoglossum pilosum
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum pilosum belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. It is a species of fern in the genus Elaphoglossum, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. The genus Elaphoglossum is part of the order Polypodiales, which consists of various species of ferns. Elaphoglossum pilosum is native to tropical regions, where its classification as a epiphytic or terrestrial fern can vary depending on its specific growth habits. The classification of Elaphoglossum pilosum is also influenced by its distribution across various countries in Central and South America.

intro: Elaphoglossum pilosum is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and hairy stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and promote overall well-being.
